event.summary: Recorded passing validation; acceptance remains a separate explicit transition.

acceptance.proof: A public project_metadata_tree_to_xml_element_tree function consumes an accepted MetadataTree plus caller-selected MetadataXmlProjectionLimits and returns an accepted XmlElementTree or MetadataXmlProjectionError. It preserves node count, caller order, parent indexes, and root identity without a synthetic wrapper. None maps to a name-only container; Some(value), including Some(empty), maps to an accepted leaf. No reverse, borrowed, TryFrom, decoding, or hidden-default projection is exposed.

None and Some(empty) remain distinct as container versus empty represented leaf. A None node with zero children remains a container, a valued childless root may become a leaf root, and the first later child that names a value-bearing parent rejects with stable parent and first-child indexes. The classification is explicitly Rusty LSL local policy rather than observed liblsl or document behavior.

Target node-count rejection precedes semantic shape scanning and allocation; the first value-bearing parent in child order precedes one fallible exact output-arena reservation; nodes then project in caller order through name validation, optional value-text validation, and character-data encoding before final XmlElementTree validation. Indexed XmlNameError, XmlTextError, XmlCharacterDataError, allocation failure, and unchanged delegated XmlElementTreeError payloads are typed and non-panicking.

The source MetadataTree is consumed. One separately allocated target candidate-node Vec is fallibly reserved because source and target node types differ. Parent order is preserved, each successful name String moves without replacing its allocation, raw value Strings validate through temporary XmlText, and XmlCharacterData uses its accepted represented-string allocation policy. The projection performs no clone, exposes no mutable target access, and delegates accepted hierarchy ownership to XmlElementTree.

A separate independently authored LSLC-001F local-results overlay binds only to accepted CORE-004 and LSLC-001B through LSLC-001E contracts. Every LSLC-001A oracle and candidate role remains not-observed with null evidence; None/Some classification, represented character data, and all failure precedence remain local candidate policy with no reverse, round-trip, info, desc, stream-info, serialization, endpoint, or official-liblsl meaning.

The crate remains dependency-, feature-, unsafe-, I/O-, activation-, decoder-, parser-, serializer-, protocol-, wire-, runtime-, FFI-, adapter-, provider-, and device-free; historical evidence remains unchanged and the feature/effect lock remains empty and inert.

document.commit_policy: Commit the one-way MetadataTree-to-XmlElementTree projection separately from reverse conversion, decoding, stream-info or field projection, document production, serialization, oracle capture, protocol, transport, adapters, providers, or runtime work.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the consuming one-way projection, exact None/Some classification, fallible allocation, and strict no-reverse/no-document/no-LSL-mapping boundary.
Describe the partial consuming projection, its local classification and allocation semantics, and the absence of reverse, round-trip, document, stream-info, or runtime meaning.

Review whether a consuming lossy representation boundary adds reusable authority or allocation guidance; retain the installed skill unchanged if its existing conversion, evidence, and ownership rules are sufficient.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review the clean-source, public-evidence, and future adapter boundary for the local projection; retain the installed router unchanged if its current rules cover the unit.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: XmlElementTree-to-MetadataTree conversion, character-reference or entity decoding, logical-text recovery, bidirectional or round-trip claims, borrowed projection, TryFrom or From defaults, source recovery on failure, or hidden limit policy.

Mixed content, valued containers, synthetic wrapper roots, ordered text/child interleaving, attributes, namespaces, declarations, comments, processing instructions, CDATA sections, schemas, queries, entities beyond the accepted LSLC-001C representation policy, or general XML DOM behavior.

Document production, well-formed-document claims, start/end/empty-tag spelling, caller-order serialization meaning, whitespace layout, parsing, mutation, serialization, canonicalization, raw bytes, endpoint representation, or exact liblsl output.

StreamDefinition or StreamDescriptor projection, channels, core/info/desc roles, stream-info meaning, LSL field mapping, Morphospace authority, commands, sessions, streams, topology, identity, permission, or platform lifecycle.

Oracle capture, candidate differential comparison, official-liblsl behavior or compatibility claims, protocol versions, wire framing, discovery, resolution, networking, sockets, inlets, outlets, transport, buffering, queues, threads, clocks, recovery, providers, FFI, native loading, adapters, or language bindings.

Cargo dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, additional crates, generated protocol material, copied or translated liblsl or rLSL source, production liblsl integration, device validation, or runtime authority.
document.objective: Implement one dependency-free, consuming, explicitly bounded, fallible projection from the accepted generic MetadataTree model into the accepted XmlElementTree container/leaf hierarchy, with exact local None/Some classification, indexed component and semantic failures, deliberate allocation ownership, and no reverse conversion, decoding, document, stream-info, protocol, wire, or runtime behavior.
outputs: A private metadata_xml_projection module with public MetadataXmlProjectionLimits, MetadataXmlProjectionError, and project_metadata_tree_to_xml_element_tree APIs that consume accepted MetadataTree state and delegate final acceptance to XmlElementTree.

Positive and damaged tests proving exact None-to-container and Some-including-empty-to-leaf classification, valued-parent rejection, XML component validation, fixed precedence, target-limit delegation, allocation behavior, and absence of reverse or hidden-default surfaces.

A separate LSLC-001F local-results overlay, deterministic focused checker, aggregate-gate routing, and public documentation that labels the classification as local policy and preserves every corpus, oracle, serialization, LSL-mapping, and authority boundary.
